paper_title: Diatom Collection and Processing in Mount Lofty Ranges

methods.description: Process for collecting diatom samples from streams using frayed ropes, rocks, and mud to study diatom colonization.

steps.description: Deploy frayed ropes in streams between 19th October and 2nd November, 2015, avoiding areas of extreme shade and/or turbulence.
steps.searchable_summary: deploy frayed ropes in streams for diatom collection

steps.description: Retrieve ropes and collect the frayed 5 cm end of each rope for analysis between 19th November and 4th December 2015.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve ropes and collect frayed ends for diatom analysis

steps.description: Collect mud samples by scraping 5 cm³ of sediment from the top of the river bed from within a 10 cm² area directly into a sampling tube.
steps.searchable_summary: collect mud samples from river bed

steps.description: Collect rock samples by rinsing rocks in the stream and scraping a 5 cm² area on the upper face of the rock.
Add methylated spirit to samples immediately after collection to preserve collected diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve diatom samples with methylated spirit

methods.description: Laboratory process for preparing and analyzing diatom samples to identify species and count diatom valves.

steps.description: Heat diatom samples in a 50-ml centrifuge tube in a water bath for 3 h at 60-80°C in 25% hydrogen peroxide to remove organic material.
steps.searchable_summary: remove organic material from diatom samples using hydrogen peroxide

steps.description: Wash samples three times in distilled water and remove the rope from the sample after rinsing.
steps.searchable_summary: wash diatom samples and remove rope

steps.description: Place high (200 µl) and low (100 µl) concentration of each sample on a coverslip and mount on slides using Naphrax.
steps.searchable_summary: mount diatom samples on slides with Naphrax

steps.description: Count approximately 300 valves on each slide using a Nikon eclipse E600 microscope at 1500x magnification.
Identify diatoms to species level using reference materials.
steps.searchable_summary: identify diatom species using reference materials

methods.description: Collection of habitat and water chemistry data to assess the major chemical, physical, and biological characteristics influencing diatom distribution.

steps.description: Measure physical and chemical parameters using a Yellow Springs Instrument multimeter in a representative section of the stream.
Collect a composite water sample from 5 to 6 representative locations in a 100 m reach at each site.
steps.searchable_summary: collect composite water sample from stream

steps.description: Store water samples on ice until receipt by the laboratory for later analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: store water samples on ice for laboratory analysis
